title: dortmanna erecta
intro: Dortmanna erecta, also known as the Marsh St. John's Wort, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, wounds, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Marsh St. John's Wort include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Marsh St. John's Wort can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support skin health and alleviate symptoms of minor skin conditions.
